intent: Reduce production incident response time so the team can recover faster from outages.
rationale: Concrete metric (MTTR), specific scope (production incidents), measurable target.

intent: Win more enterprise deals in the EMEA region this quarter.
rationale: Specific segment (enterprise EMEA), implies measurable outcomes (deals closed).

intent: Improve the new-user onboarding flow so more trial users reach activation in their first week.
rationale: Specific cohort (new users), concrete activation metric, time-bounded (first week).

intent: Build awareness in the SMB segment by establishing a content distribution presence.
rationale: Segment is named, mechanism (content) is concrete, awareness is measurable via reach.

intent: Reduce monthly cloud spend without sacrificing system reliability.
rationale: Two competing constraints clearly stated; both can be quantified.

intent: Increase renewal rate among mid-market customers by addressing churn drivers identified in Q1.
rationale: Specific segment, references prior insight, implies quantifiable target (renewal rate).

intent: Migrate three legacy services off the deprecated authentication library before its end-of-support date in September.
rationale: Concrete count (three services), specific deadline, clear success criterion.

intent: Improve engineering team retention by addressing the top three exit-interview themes from the past two quarters.
rationale: Measurable outcome (retention rate), concrete inputs (themes), bounded scope.

intent: Roll out self-serve analytics to product managers across the organisation by end of quarter.
rationale: Audience defined, deliverable concrete, time-bounded.

intent: Tighten quarterly forecast accuracy so revenue projections fall within plus or minus five percent of actuals.
rationale: Quantitative threshold built into the intent itself.

intent: Make the product better.
rationale: No specificity, no audience, no metric. Classic 'better' without a what.

intent: Become a leader in our space through high-quality content.
rationale: 'Leader' is vague, 'high-quality' is unfalsifiable. Critic should flag both phrases.

intent: Optimise our infrastructure to enable scaling.
rationale: Two vague verbs (optimise, enable), no metric, no scale target.

intent: Drive significant revenue growth by leveraging our world-class sales team.
rationale: Three weasel words: significant, leveraging, world-class. No baseline, no target.

intent: Improve operational excellence across the organisation.
rationale: 'Excellence' has no metric. Scope ('the organisation') is too broad to be actionable.

intent: Foster a stronger engineering culture.
rationale: 'Culture' is unmeasurable as stated. Needs concrete behaviours or signals.

intent: Make sure customers are happy.
rationale: Happiness has proxies (NPS, CSAT, renewal) but they are not named.

intent: Build out the platform vision.
rationale: Aspirational without measurable outcomes; 'vision' is leadership-speak, not OKR material.

intent: Adopt best practices across the codebase.
rationale: 'Best practices' is unfalsifiable; 'adopt' has no completion criterion.

intent: Strengthen financial discipline.
rationale: 'Strengthen' and 'discipline' are unmeasurable as stated. Needs cost or accuracy metrics.

intent: Be the best.
rationale: Too short to convey any intent. Cannot be drafted into a meaningful OKR.

intent: asdf jkl test
rationale: Garbage input — placeholder text. Drafter should escalate.

intent: Maintain the current status quo.
rationale: Anti-OKR — explicitly seeks zero change. Defeats the purpose of OKRs.

intent: Hire more people.
rationale: Activity, not outcome. 'Hire more' is a means; the OKR should target what hiring achieves.

intent: Make money.
rationale: Too generic to be drafted into a measurable OKR — 'making money' covers the entire business.

intent: Whatever the CEO wants.
rationale: Process-of-elimination intent — not a goal, just a deferral.

intent: Do good work.
rationale: Aspirational platitude with no surface for measurement.

intent: Be more data-driven.
rationale: Adjective without a target state. Needs specific decisions or metrics moved by data.

intent: Avoid losing money.
rationale: Negation; impossible to score 0.7. Reframe as a positive outcome.

intent: Don't let customers churn.
rationale: Negation again. Reframe as a retention rate target.
